description: Deprecated plan operators detected in EXPLAIN output for Gate 3 quality validation. Updated per Neo4j release.

operators.name: VarLengthExpand(Pruning)
operators.reason: Produced by deprecated [:REL*] variable-length path syntax; replace with QPE {:m,n} form

operators.name: VarLengthExpand(PruningBFS)
operators.reason: Produced by deprecated shortestPath() / allShortestPaths() functions; replace with SHORTEST / ALL SHORTEST

operators.name: RelationshipCountFromCountStore
operators.reason: Triggered by count(()-[:REL]-()) which is deprecated; use COUNT { MATCH ()-[:REL]-() } subquery form

operators.name: NodeCountFromCountStore
operators.reason: Triggered by count(n) pattern which is deprecated in some contexts; use COUNT { MATCH (n) } if needed

operators.name: CartesianProduct
operators.reason: Unconstrained cross-join of two or more node sets; add WHERE predicate to relate them or use CALL subquery

operators.name: AllNodesScan
operators.reason: Full graph scan — no label or index predicate on this node; add label and ensure index exists

notes: Operator names are matched case-insensitively as substrings of the EXPLAIN plan text.
FAIL-grade operators cause a Gate 3 FAIL; WARN-grade operators cause a Gate 3 WARN.
AllNodesScan and CartesianProduct are not deprecated per se but indicate fundamental query authoring errors.
Source: Neo4j query plan documentation and observed plan operator names in Neo4j 2025.x / 2026.x.
